[english] How can I get a processed parse tree representation of my input source using this method?
[english] This function lazily generates the grammar structure, ensuring parsing only occurs on the first call to avoid redundant computation.
<switch:python>
[python] def grammar(self):
    """The parse tree generated by the source."""
    if self._grammar is None:
      self.parser = Parser()
      grammar = self.parser.parse(self.input_source)
      self._grammar = grammar.trimmed().flattened().flattened(self._flatten)
    return self._grammar
<switch:english>
[english] The final result involves two successive calls to `flattened()`, which suggests the data structure is being heavily normalized before use.
[english] What happens if `self._grammar` has already been computed and is not None?
[english] If a value exists in `self._grammar`, the function immediately returns that cached result, bypassing all parsing logic.
[english] That makes sense; I will rely on this method for efficiency.
